note: Small, author-curated set; weighted toward adversarial look-alikes. Not a statistical guarantee.

cases.name: login button renamed
cases.reasoning: The login button exists and is functional but its ID was renamed from 'login-btn' to 'sign-in-btn'. The button with text 'Sign in' is clearly the same login button, just with a different identifier.

cases.name: add-to-cart id -> data-testid
cases.reasoning: The 'Add to cart' button is present and functional, but its ID selector '#add-to-cart' no longer matches. The element is now identified by a data-testid attribute instead of an ID.

cases.name: submit class -> id
cases.reasoning: The submit button exists in the DOM but has been re-implemented with an ID attribute ('submitOrder') rather than the CSS class 'submit-btn'. The feature is intact but the selector has drifted.

cases.name: email name -> id
cases.reasoning: The email input exists but uses an id attribute instead of a name attribute. The selector drifted from input[name=email] to input#email, but the element is still present and functional.

cases.name: nav link renamed
cases.reasoning: The navigation link to Home exists in the DOM but its ID has changed from 'nav-home' to 'home-link'. The feature is intact, only the selector has drifted.

cases.name: 500 on dashboard
cases.reasoning: The page is showing a 500 Internal Server Error instead of the dashboard. This is not a selector drift issue — the application itself is broken and returning a server error, which is why the #dashboard element is not present in the DOM.

cases.name: invalid credentials
cases.reasoning: The page is showing 'Invalid credentials' instead of 'Welcome back', indicating the login attempt failed. This is not a selector drift issue — the expected success message is absent and the application is returning an authentication error.

cases.name: transfer failed (insufficient funds)

cases.name: search box name -> id
cases.reasoning: The search input field exists but the name attribute 'q' has changed. The input is now identified by id='search' rather than name='q', but the element is clearly the same search functionality.

cases.name: menu button class -> testid
cases.reasoning: The hamburger menu button still exists in the UI but the class name '.hamburger' has changed. The same functional element is now identified by 'data-testid=menu-toggle' with aria-label 'Menu'.

cases.name: [adversarial] submit renamed, unrelated promo banner
cases.reasoning: The submit button exists and is functional, but its ID changed from 'submit' to 'submit-order'. The button text 'Submit order' confirms this is the correct element, just with a drifted selector.

cases.name: [adversarial] add-to-cart present but out of stock
cases.reasoning: The product is out of stock, which is why the 'Add to cart' button is disabled. The selector drift is minor (id changed from 'add-to-cart' to 'add-to-cart-btn'), but the core issue is that the button is disabled and the item is out of stock
